verse_text: 'Naked I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return. The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away. As it seemed good to the Lord, so also it came to pass. Blessed be the name of the Lord.'

pericope.title: Job Loses His Children and Property

links.context: Ezekiel lying bound on his side images the death of Christ, the Lamb of God, who bore the acts of unrighteousness of all humanity. 4:12-17 It would have been unthinkable for Ezekiel to carry out this command as first given. The Apostle Peter made a similar protest (see [[ACT.10:9]]-16). But in a striking reverse parallel, Christ made Himself to be sin for our sakes ([[2CO.5:21]]), so that all who waste away in their wrongdoings (v. 17) might partake together of the bread of heaven and the water of eternal life. 5:1-4 The hair and its destruction by fire, sword, and wind all point ultimately to the final judgment (compare [[JOB.1:13]]-22; Is 7:20). The hair sewn into the garment illustrates the faithful remnant who have bound themselves to Christ.

symbolic.title: Example: The Cherubim of Ezekiel I
